_meta.description: CDL point estimates by violation category and state. For attorney and driver reference only — estimates, not legal advice. Verify exact values with state DMV.
_meta.source: FMCSA 49 CFR 383.51, state DMV point schedules, and CDL legal reference guides.
_meta.federal_serious_rule: 2 serious violations in any 3-year period = 60-day CDL disqualification. 3 in 3 years = 120 days. (49 CFR 383.51(c))
_meta.federal_major_rule: 1 major violation = minimum 1-year CDL disqualification. If transporting HazMat = 3 years. (49 CFR 383.51(b))
_meta.state_points_note: State points shown are CDL-effective — some states apply a multiplier for commercial drivers. School/work zone additions listed separately.

Speeding (15+).disqualification_note: Federal serious violation under 49 CFR 383.51(c). A 2nd serious violation within 3 years triggers 60-day CDL disqualification. Attorney appearance strongly recommended.

CA.note: NOTS system — CDL commercial vehicles get 1.5x weight

IL.note: IL 150-pt scale converted to standard equivalent

NY.note: NY CDL holders face stricter accumulation limits

Speeding (1-14).disqualification_note: Not a federal serious violation. State point accumulation risk — 4+ standard violations can put CDL at risk through state suspension thresholds.

Reckless Driving.disqualification_note: Federal serious violation under 49 CFR 383.51(c). Typically a criminal traffic offense. One conviction near-guarantees CDL insurance rate increase. 2nd serious violation within 3 years = 60-day CDL suspension.

Cell Phone.disqualification_note: Federal serious violation — hand-held mobile telephone use while operating a CMV (49 CFR 392.82 and 383.51(c)). Also carries federal civil penalty up to $2,750 per offense. 2nd serious violation in 3 years = 60-day CDL disqualification.

NC.note: NC: no state points for adult drivers on cell phone — federal penalty still applies

PA.note: PA: no state points for cell phone — federal penalty still applies

TX.note: TX: no state points for cell phone — federal penalty still applies

Following too close.disqualification_note: Federal serious violation under 49 CFR 383.51(c). Commonly cited after rear-end crashes. Carries extra weight if accident was involved. 2nd serious violation in 3 years = 60-day CDL disqualification.

Lane Violation.disqualification_note: Improper or erratic lane changes are a federal serious violation under 49 CFR 383.51(c). Simple lane drifting may be charged as careless driving instead. 2nd serious violation in 3 years = 60-day CDL disqualification.

Failure to yield to emergency vehicle.disqualification_note: Not a federal CDL serious violation, but elevated state point value in most states. Some states treat this as an aggravated offense with higher fines. Point accumulation risk.

Careless Driving.disqualification_note: Not a federal CDL serious violation. However, prosecutors may upgrade careless driving to reckless driving when a CDL is involved, which IS a federal serious violation. Point accumulation risk.

Failure to Obey Traffic Control Device.disqualification_note: Standard moving violation. Note: railroad-grade crossing violations (bypass, failure to stop) ARE a federal serious violation under 49 CFR 383.51(c) — escalate if the specific charge involves a railroad crossing.

Too Fast for Conditions.disqualification_note: Not a federal CDL serious violation. Often charged alongside other violations after an accident. Standard moving violation — point accumulation risk.

Seatbelt.disqualification_note: Federal CMV violation under 49 CFR 392.16 with CSA weight of 7, but NOT listed as a federal 'serious violation' under 49 CFR 383.51(c). Does not count toward 60-day CDL disqualification threshold. State points apply.

FL.note: FL: no state points for seatbelt — fine only

NJ.note: NJ: no points for seatbelt — fine only

TX.note: TX: no points for seatbelt — fine only

Alcohol / Drug related violation.disqualification_note: FEDERAL MAJOR VIOLATION under 49 CFR 383.51(b). First offense = minimum 1-year CDL disqualification (3 years if transporting HazMat). Second offense = LIFETIME CDL disqualification. Criminal charges apply. Immediate action required.

CA.note: 2 base NOTS pts × 1.5 CDL weight = 3 pts; additional revocation points applied

Driver license violation.disqualification_note: Driving while CDL is disqualified or suspended is a FEDERAL MAJOR VIOLATION (1-year CDL disqualification). No valid CDL / wrong CDL class is a federal serious violation. Immediate escalation required — severity depends on the specific charge.

ELD/Logs.disqualification_note: HOS / ELD violations are FMCSA regulatory violations, not state moving violations. No state license points in most jurisdictions. However, CSA score impact is significant and multiple HOS violations can lead to carrier audit. Out-of-service order possible.

Equipment/Maintenance.disqualification_note: Equipment violations are typically regulatory/administrative. No state license points in most jurisdictions. CSA Vehicle Maintenance BASIC impacted. Out-of-service possible for critical safety violations (brakes, steering, tires).

Registration Violations.disqualification_note: Administrative violation. No state license points in most jurisdictions. Typically fines only. No federal CDL impact unless the registration violation is a federal FMCSA compliance issue.

Overweight/Overlength.disqualification_note: State regulatory violation. Fines are typically per-pound-over with significant financial exposure. No state license points in most jurisdictions. No federal CDL disqualification risk. Carrier liability may apply.

Parking.disqualification_note: Administrative violation. No state license points in any jurisdiction. No CDL impact unless the parking violation involves a CMV parked illegally in a hazardous location (which may trigger an inspection report).
